domainMap: Center: Building / Space / Store (mass-market retailer physical stores & real estate, next decade, esp. AI). Branches: Labor/Work & Operations; Supply Chain; Infrastructure (energy/water/compute, parking, location, layout); Construction (methods, materials, trades); Retail Industry; Tech (AI, physical AI, robotics, computer vision, automation); People & Human System (commerce, transaction, discovery, community, experience, mobility, demographic shifts, migration); Policy/Regulation/Finance (zoning, insurance, regions); Sustainability & Environment (heat, climate events, circular).  ===HUNT BRIEF (READ CAREFULLY)=== The daily feeds skew toward near-term (H1/H2) trade news; your job is to fight that. STRONGLY prefer H3 (10-30 year), civilizational, GLOBAL, and WEAK material. Set horizon to H3 whenever plausible. Reward: (1) non-Western and peripheral-geography signals (Asia, Africa, Gulf, Latin America, small jurisdictions); (2) second-order and third-order implications nobody has drawn for the built environment; (3) reframings and ABSENCES (a category/rule/doctrine that does NOT yet exist); (4) items from science, subculture, or fringe sources, not business press; (5) developments that would sound absurd or unsettling to a retail executive today. Demote on sight: quarterly earnings, store-count/closure tallies, named-analyst trend decks, product launches, anything already coined and widely covered — include at most 1-2 such as classification 'Trend'. A candidate that merely restates a trade headline is a FAILURE. Push every candidate to its strangest, most distant, most global reading that still ties — however loosely — to the future of physical stores and real estate. Add a 'horizon' field ('H1'|'H2'|'H3') to every candidate.
gdeltQueries: extreme heat night economy business hours
